title: Fixing React Memoized Component Re-renders Due to Changing Array Depth

statement.markdown: Given a React component with memoization, fix the implementation to prevent unnecessary re-renders when the array depth changes. The component should only re-render when the actual array elements change.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a JavaScript object with an array property.
statement.notes: Time and space complexity should be optimized.

examples.explanation: The component should re-render when the array elements change.
The component should not re-render when the array depth changes.

hints.text: Use a library like `lodash.isequal` to compare arrays.

editorial.title: Editorial Solution
editorial.intuition: Memoization in React can be tricky when dealing with complex data structures like arrays.
editorial.bruteForce: A naive approach would be to compare the entire array on every render, which is inefficient.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a library like `lodash.isequal` to compare arrays and prevent unnecessary re-renders.
editorial.optimalSolution: Implement a custom array comparison function to handle changes in array depth.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The component should only re-render when the actual array elements change.
